theme.name: broadway_boogie
theme.notes: Inspired by Mondrian's Broadway Boogie Woogie (1942-43) and its grid of primary color blocks on a yellow ground. Key decisions: F Mixolydian means I7 = F-A-C-Eb — dominant-seventh quality on the tonic. This chord never resolves. It is a color field, not a function. The flat-7 (Eb) gives momentum without resolution anxiety. No bVII, no IV, no modulation. The grid IS the composition; only density, register, and tempo change. Correction: II and V as originally notated implied major qualities requiring B-natural and E-natural, neither in F Mixolydian. Diatonic ii (Gm) and v (Cm) are minor -- use lowercase Roman numerals throughout, plus iii° (Adim/half-dim, passing) and vi (Dm). The vi-ii-v-I descending-fourth chain is the primary harmonic vehicle for high-density movements: functional-sounding motion built entirely from diatonic chords, so it drives without ever resolving to a leading tone. bVII (Eb) is reserved for lower-density/color-block movements (Mondrian color-field concept).
